title: phebalium glandulosum subsp. glandulosum
intro: Phebalium glandulosum subsp. glandulosum, also known as the Rough-leaved Phebalium, is a plant species within the Rutaceae family. It is native to eastern Australia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, and its extracts have been shown to have antioxidant and immune-boosting properties, providing health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Phebalium glandulosum subsp. glandulosum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
